quantity: share of introduced bills that become law in recent U.S. Congresses
source: Congress.gov bulk data and govinfo BILLSTATUS collections
notes: Screening range tightened around the committed bill-progression raw sample while retaining abstraction margin

quantity: share of introduced bills receiving floor consideration or final chamber action
source: Congress.gov bulk data and govinfo BILLSTATUS collections
notes: Tightened screen to keep the benchmark from treating every introduced bill as a floor bill

quantity: ordinary enacted-bill coalition support range under a polarized Congress
notes: Screens winning coalition size against the committed Voteview roll-call sample

dataset: Congress.gov veto actions and CRS presidential veto summaries
quantity: vetoes per simulated run under bicameral presidential-veto baseline
source: Congress.gov action histories and CRS presidential veto summaries
notes: Loose range because run length is abstract

dataset: Center for Effective Lawmaking and Congress.gov sponsor samples
quantity: concentration in member-level legislative advancement and sponsor proposal access
source: Center for Effective Lawmaking and Congress.gov sponsor samples
notes: Screens whether proposer access is neither perfectly equal nor fully concentrated

dataset: U.S. Senate Lobbying Disclosure Act data
quantity: organized-interest spending should be visible in explicit lobbying scenarios
source: U.S. Senate Lobbying Disclosure Act filings
notes: Abstract budget-unit observability check rather than dollar calibration

quantity: policy-topic throughput should not collapse to zero in conventional baselines
notes: Coarse screen for generated issue-domain throughput before topic-specific calibration
